Occupational Therapist License in West Virginia

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$0
Processing time
4 to 6 Weeks
Renewal fee
$66
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
West Virginia Board of Occupational Therapy
Path
OT Compact enacted
See the steps Primary board page

Last updated 2026-03-22.

Out-of-State Occupational Therapists

West Virginia has enacted the OT Compact, but the compact is not live for clinicians yet. For now, out-of-state applicants still use endorsement.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in West Virginia.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside West Virginia can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: West Virginia requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: OT Compact is enacted in West Virginia, but it is not issuing privileges yet.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

West Virginia Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed occupational therapists applying to practice in West Virginia.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the West Virginia Board of Occupational Therapy. The application fee is N/A.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    West Virginia requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Request Official Transcripts

    Sent directly from your educational institution.

  4. Step 4

    Submit Your NBCOT Score Report

    Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

  5. Step 5

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    West Virginia requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects). Completing prints outside West Virginia can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  6. Step 6

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your West Virginia Occupational Therapist license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
N/A
Background check
$0 (included in application) (The board's processing fee is included in the application, but out-of-state fingerprinting services like IdentoGO charge a separate fee of ~$40)
Total estimated cost
$150 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
4 to 6 Weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$66

Temporary License

West Virginia offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

West Virginia Occupational Therapist Licensing FAQ

How much does a West Virginia OT license cost?

The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

Is NBCOT certification required?

Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

Does West Virginia have reciprocity for occupational therapists?

West Virginia has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to West Virginia?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in West Virginia while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
